name: Childrens Health Defense
description: Childrens Health Defense (CHD), founded by Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is an American anti-vaccine advocacy group. CHD promotes misinformation and conspiracy theories about vaccines, 5G technology, and alleged links between environmental toxins and chronic diseases. It claims that vaccines cause autism and other health problems, despite overwhelming scientific evidence to the contrary. CHD alleges a widespread conspiracy involving pharmaceutical companies, government agencies, and mainstream media to suppress information about vaccine dangers and promote vaccination for profit. The organization has been criticized for spreading false and misleading information, contributing to vaccine hesitancy, and endangering public health. Mainstream science and medical organizations, including the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO), firmly refute CHD's claims about vaccine safety and efficacy. They emphasize the rigorous testing and monitoring processes for vaccines and the overwhelming evidence supporting their benefits in preventing infectious diseases. Critics argue that CHD cherry-picks data, misrepresents scientific studies, and relies on anecdotal evidence to support its claims. Furthermore, it has been accused of profiting from the sale of books and supplements that promote its anti-vaccine agenda.
subtitle: Anti-vaccine advocacy and misinformation.

claims.evidence: Cited studies retracted or debunked; anecdotal reports.
claims.criticism: Numerous studies have found no link between vaccines and autism. The original study linking MMR vaccine to autism was retracted due to fraudulent data.

claims.claim: 5G technology is harmful to human health.
claims.evidence: Cited studies with questionable methodology; correlation misinterpreted as causation.
claims.criticism: Major scientific reviews have found no evidence that 5G poses a health risk to humans at the levels of exposure currently experienced.

claims.claim: Pharmaceutical companies and government agencies are conspiring to hide vaccine dangers.
claims.evidence: Selective interpretation of data; distrust of authority; reliance on 'insider' accounts.
claims.criticism: This claim lacks credible evidence and relies on conspiracy thinking. Regulatory agencies exist to ensure vaccine safety and efficacy.
